content:
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which moved story-09 task 03 out of the active queue and into the done summary.
- Committed the task-specific README/test changes plus Ralph bookkeeping as `5b941f7` with full validation evidence in the commit message.
- Next I am pushing `master` and then checking the remaining worktree dirt so I can report only the intentional unrelated leftovers.

- Read previous progress, refreshed Ralph summaries, and loaded the choose-task rules plus the list-active-tasks skill workflow.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, no visible active task priority tags, and no visible active blocker tags in the remaining queue.
- Inspected the actual repo shape: Rust workspace (`runner`, `source-bootstrap`, `ingest-contract`), README command contract, Cargo manifests, and the existing Docker-backed E2E harness in `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_harness.rs`.
- Compared the first tasks in stories 11 through 15. The most logical next task is story 11 task 01 because it directly extends the existing E2E migration harness and completed baseline E2E story with externally imposed retry/failure coverage before higher-level integrity audits, novice-user verification, reports, or CI publishing.
- Next if resuming: execute story-11 task 01 with TDD and validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all pass.

- Read previous codex progress and confirmed the next active task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions for this turn.
- Next: read the story-11 task file, inspect repo state around the E2E harness, and determine whether I must plan-only or execute.

- Read the active task file for story 11 task 01.
- The task is still in planning state (`not_started`, no `NOW EXECUTE` marker), so per instructions this turn must create a separate plan file and stop.
- Next: inspect the existing E2E harness, webhook runtime, and nearby Ralph planning conventions so the plan is specific about interfaces, TDD slices, and code-boundary cleanup.

- Read the existing long-lane harness, default-bootstrap harness, webhook runtime, webhook persistence, and the end-to-end test strategy doc.
- The likely clean design is an external test-side HTTPS chaos front door that returns 500s before forwarding to the real runner, so retries are imposed outside the binary and duplicate delivery/idempotency are exercised through the shipped path.
- Next: inspect whether the current harness already exposes a clean insertion point for a proxy/failure gate, then write the plan file, add its path to the task markdown, and stop.

- Created the story-11 task-01 plan at .ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/01-task-e2e-http-retry-chaos-imposed-externally_plans/2026-04-19-http-retry-chaos-e2e-plan.md and linked it from the task markdown.
- The plan intentionally keeps product CLIs unchanged and proposes a test-side HTTPS chaos gateway that forwards to the real runner while selectively returning 500s outward, so duplicate delivery and helper-shadow idempotency are exercised without binary test hooks.
- The plan ends in NOW EXECUTE. Next turn should read the plan, execute strict TDD vertical slices, keep chaos support outside product code, and continue until make check/lint/test/test-long all pass.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is story-11 task 01 and prior context says its separate execution plan already exists and ended in NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task markdown and the linked plan so I can execute the work under the existing design instead of re-planning.

- Read the task markdown and the execution plan, which ends in NOW EXECUTE.
- Inspected the default long-lane test plus the generic and default-bootstrap E2E support; the current generic harness conflates the runner bind address with the public Cockroach webhook sink address.
- Next I am tracing config generation and runner startup in `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_harness.rs` so I can start with a real RED slice that separates those boundaries and enables an external HTTPS chaos gateway.

- Ran the existing ignored baseline default-bootstrap long-lane test directly; it passed in about 7 seconds, so the current real E2E path is healthy enough to extend.
- Confirmed the next boundary work is feasible: inspect runner test dependencies plus the existing TLS asset helpers in `e2e_harness.rs` before adding the first RED retry-chaos scenario.
- Next I will add the ignored long-lane tracer-bullet test and run only that case to get a real failing signal before implementing the gateway support.

- Executed the retry-chaos plan in vertical slices against the real ignored long-lane test.
- Added a dedicated HTTPS `webhook_chaos_gateway` support module, separated the generic harness's runner listener from the source webhook sink URL, and extended the default customer harness with retry-chaos scenario helpers.
- The new ignored long-lane scenario is green end-to-end: one external 500 is imposed on the updated-customer batch, the gateway observes duplicate delivery, helper shadow converges idempotently, destination tables stabilize, and `runner verify` stays on the public CLI path without helper-table leakage.
- Next I am running repository-wide validation starting with formatting/lint, then `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, fixing the first failing lane at a time.
